_comment: Add these hook entries to your ~/.claude/settings.json hooks section. Replace YOUR_MEMORY_DIR with your actual memory path (same as MEMORY_DIR in config.sh).

hooks.command: MEMORY_DIR="YOUR_MEMORY_DIR"; GRAPH="$MEMORY_DIR/graphify-out/graph.json"; FLAG="$HOME/.claude/graphify-needs-rebuild.flag"; if [ -f "$GRAPH" ] && find "$MEMORY_DIR" -maxdepth 1 -name "*.md" -newer "$GRAPH" | grep -q .; then touch "$FLAG"; fi

[ -f $HOME/.claude/graphify-needs-rebuild.flag ] && echo '{"hookSpecificOutput":{"hookEventName":"SessionStart","additionalContext":"Memory graph is stale — new memory files added since last graphify run. Consider running /graphify on your memory folder this session."}}' || true

[ -f graphify-out/graph.json ] && echo '{"hookSpecificOutput":{"hookEventName":"PreToolUse","additionalContext":"graphify: Knowledge graph exists. Read graphify-out/GRAPH_REPORT.md for community structure before searching raw files."}}' || true
